ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined (tails optional)
1/2 cup cornstarch or potato starch (for dredging)
1 tsp baking powder (optional for extra lightness)
Neutral oil for deep-frying (enough for 2-3 inches depth)
steps: Pat shrimp very dry. If desired, make a few shallow cuts on the belly side so they fry straight.
Heat oil to about 350-375 F (175-190 C) in a deep pot.
Make batter: lightly whisk egg and ice-cold water, then gently stir in flour (and baking powder if using). Do not overmix; a few small lumps are fine.
Dredge shrimp in cornstarch, shake off excess, then dip in batter.
Fry in small batches 2-3 minutes until pale golden and crisp. Drain on a rack or paper towels; season with a pinch of salt.
tips: Keep batter cold and mix minimally to limit gluten; avoid crowding so oil temperature stays steady.

sources.title: Tempura Recipe (Japanese Deep Fried Vegetables and Shrimp)
